# Wahb Amir — Full-Stack Web & AI Developer

> Wahb Amir is a 16-year-old self-taught full-stack developer and AI developer based in Pakistan. He builds production-ready web applications, backend services, and AI-powered systems end-to-end — from idea to deployed product. In March 2026 he placed 3rd out of 775 international participants at Hack for Humanity 2026 with EcoLens, an AI waste classifier built entirely solo in one month. In April 2026 he won the Technical Award at Hackonomics 2026 out of 1481 international participants with EconoQuest — also built solo on zero cloud budget. He manages his own Linux servers and has shipped multiple deployed production projects within his first year of coding.

## Key Facts

- Age: 16 · Self-taught · Coding seriously for just over one year
- Nationality: Pakistan
- Primary stack: Next.js, TypeScript, Fastify, Supabase, pgvector, Python, LangChain, Groq, PyTorch
- 3rd Place — Hack for Humanity 2026 (775 international participants, solo) with [EcoLens](https://eco.wahb.space)
- Technical Award — Hackonomics 2026 (1481 international participants, solo) with [EconoQuest](https://econoquest.wahb.space)
- Manages own Linux servers, deploys production apps independently

## Projects

- [ClearPath — AI Document Intelligence Platform](https://clearpath.wahb.space): USAII 2026 submission. An AI-powered document intelligence platform that processes complex official documents through a 5-stage LLM pipeline, providing plain-English summaries, tracked deadlines, and extracted action items via real-time SSE. [Case Study](https://wahb.space/projects/clearpath) · [Code](https://github.com/wahb-amir/clearpath)
- [EconoQuest — AI Economics Simulator](https://econoquest.wahb.space): Technical Award winner at Hackonomics 2026 (1481 participants). Browser-based economics simulation with full RAG advisory pipeline (99 chunks, 5 layers), Supabase pgvector, Groq Llama 3.3 70B, Fastify gateway, WebSocket streaming, pure-Python conflict detector, 7-archetype system, zero cloud spend. [Case Study](https://wahb.space/projects/econoquest) · [Frontend Code](https://github.com/wahb-amir/EconoQuest) · [Backend Code](https://github.com/wahb-amir/EconoQuestBackend)
- [EcoLens — AI Waste Classifier](https://eco.wahb.space): 3rd place at Hack for Humanity 2026 (775 participants). AI-powered waste classifier, point a camera at any waste item to receive classification, CO₂/water/energy impact, and gamified points. Built solo in one month on zero paid API budget. [Case Study](https://wahb.space/projects/ecolens) · [Code](https://github.com/wahb-amir/ecolens)
- [Client & Developer Collaboration Platform](https://dashboard.wahb.space): Unified workspace where clients request quotes, track progress, message developers, and see real-time GitHub-synced updates. GitHub Actions + webhooks automatically map commits and PRs to project tasks. [Case Study](https://wahb.space/projects/client-dev-platform) · [Code](https://github.com/wahb-amir/dashboard)
- [Modern Online Store (BoltForm)](https://boltform.wahb.space): Performant e-commerce demo with secure Stripe checkout, mobile-first UX, and a read-only admin dashboard. [Code](https://github.com/wahb-amir/Boltform)

## Tech Stack

- **Frontend:** HTML, CSS, Tailwind, JavaScript, TypeScript, React, Next.js, Framer Motion
- **Backend:** Node.js, Fastify, Express, Python, Pandas, NumPy, PyTorch
- **AI/ML:** LangChain, HuggingFace, Groq, RAG, pgvector, computer vision, lightweight model deployment
- **Databases:** MongoDB, PostgreSQL, Supabase, pgvector, Redis
- **DevOps:** Docker, NGINX, GitHub Actions, Linux VPS
- **Other:** WebSocket, SWR, JWT, SMTP, Mongoose
